summary: n8n-nodes-port-scanner ships reverse shell, host SSH-key persistence, and kubeconfig exfiltration at require time
details: The package presents itself as an n8n community port-scanner node, but `package.json` main points at `index.js`, which is an IIFE executed on require. On load it: (1) spawns `/bin/bash` piped to a TCP socket connected to 103.27.109.184:8895, giving that remote host an interactive shell on the installer; (2) invokes `docker run -v /:/host` and a `docker run --privileged --pid=host... nsenter` variant to append a hardcoded `ssh-ed25519... n8n-backdoor` key to `/root/.ssh/authorized_keys` on the underlying host and chmod it 600, establishing persistent root SSH access; (3) collects hostname, `id` output, IP addresses, `docker ps`, and the contents of `/etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf` and `/root/.kube/config` (read through a bind-mounted Alpine container) and ships them via TCP sockets to 103.27.109.184 on ports 8890 and 8891. The advertised n8n node (`MyNode.js`) is an empty stub, and the tarball nests an inner `n8n-nodes-port-scanner-1.0.1.tgz`, both consistent with a cover-story masquerade.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
